text: Fill in the blanks about gametogenesis and fertilisation
questions: The endometrium has two layers: the *functionalis/functional* layer that is shed during menstruation, and the *basalis/basal* layer that serves as the regenerative source.
The LH surge triggers *ovulation/ovulate* on approximately day *14/fourteen* of a 28-day menstrual cycle, and the ruptured follicle transforms into the corpus *luteum/luteal*.
Spermatogenesis occurs in the *seminiferous/seminiferous tubules* tubules and takes approximately *74/seventy-four* days to complete from spermatogonium to mature spermatozoon.
Fertilisation normally occurs in the *ampulla/ampullary* region of the fallopian tube, and the *acrosome/acrosomal* reaction enables the sperm to penetrate the zona pellucida.
